title: City of Berkeley Adopted Capital Improvement Program FY2025-FY2029

verbatim_unfunded_needs.quote: Beyond the projects included in this five-year CIP, the unfunded capital needs total approximately $1.65 billion. Long term funding strategies will need to be developed in the future to meet these infrastructure needs and options will be explored with the City Council and the community.
verbatim_unfunded_needs.location: Executive Summary, Challenges and Opportunities, p. 13

property_transfer_tax_impact.description: Excess PTT projections down for FY2025 and FY2026, reducing capital funding vs. FY2023-2024.
property_transfer_tax_impact.note: The FY2023-2024 capital infusion was explicitly one-time (driven by elevated PTT). FY2025-2026 reverts toward baseline.

five_year_street_rehabilitation_plan.note: A $92M, 5-year paving commitment exists — but this addresses only a portion of the $1.65B unfunded backlog.

deferred_maintenance_cost_multiplier.verbatim: Studies show that $1 spent in early maintenance of infrastructure, such as streets, can save $7 in later, more expensive repairs. This explains why delays in addressing deferred maintenance in the City's streets quadruples the cost of addressing these needs by 2050.
deferred_maintenance_cost_multiplier.scoring_significance: The CIP explicitly states that delaying maintenance quadruples costs by 2050 — yet the unfunded backlog sits at $1.65B unchanged. The council approves CIPs that acknowledge this ratio while not funding the backlog.

scoring_implications: The $1.65B unfunded capital needs figure is identical to the FY2023-2027 CIP — zero progress despite two bond measures (Measure M and Measure T1) and one full budget cycle.
The executive summary explicitly states capital funding is REDUCED in FY2025-2026 vs. FY2023-2024 due to declining PTT — the backlog will grow, not shrink.

The 1:7 maintenance ratio and '4x cost by 2050' language appear in a document adopted by the same council that is advancing a new bond measure. The council cannot claim ignorance of the backlog's trajectory.
